item.title: Interstate fans say keep Gather Round in Adelaide as AFL boss probed on why AFLW not part of the program
item.description: Crows AFLW player Ebony Marinoff has questioned the AFL's CEO on why the women's program hasn't been part of the successful Gather Round in Adelaide.

item.title: Queen Mary of Denmark's father dies in Tasmania aged 84
item.description: The Australian-born Queen of Denmark last visited her father Professor John Donaldson during a royal visit to Tasmania last month.

item.title: Legendary Indian Bollywood playback singer Asha Bhosle dies, aged 92
item.description: The singer recorded over 12,000 songs and was one of Indian cinema's most celebrated voices.

item.title: Rory McIlroy joins exclusive group with second straight Masters victory
item.description: Rory McIlroy becomes just the fourth man in history to win back-to-back Masters titles after holding off a star-studded field in the final round at Augusta.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 13: Jump in, we're going back in time
item.description: Which state had Australia's only communist MP? And where did the Titanic sink?

item.title: The Melbourne project turning used tennis balls into shoes
item.description: What should be done with all those used tennis balls that get inevitably sent to landfill? Lilian Xu might have found the answer.

item.title: There's a case for nationalising child care, but of course it won't happen
item.description: Australia definitely won't nationalise child care, even though the data suggests there's a strong case. Perhaps a more feasible idea would be to copy what Canada has done.

item.title: Behind solar power's meteoric rise is a little-known Australian
item.description: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

item.title: The 'extraordinary' rise of Kim Jong Un's daughter, who's tipped to be North Korea's next leader
item.description: North Korea leader Kim Jong Un's 13-year-old daughter is tipped to be next in line. So who is Kim Ju Ae?

item.title: Fuel crisis creating COVID-esque shocks for workers and businesses
item.description: The COVID-19 pandemic brought job losses, working from home and economic instability. Some fear the US-Israel war on Iran and spiking fuel prices have created similar struggles.

item.title: Tasmanian tourist attraction West Coast Wilderness Railway facing uncertain future
item.description: The West Coast Wilderness Railway in Tasmania is a beloved attraction and an important employer. The state government says it wants the railway to have a sustainable future, but hasn't said how that could be achieved.

item.title: Victorian government's Big Housing Build faced budget blowout in early days of landmark program
item.description: Internal documents the Victorian government tried to withhold reveal that a landmark social housing construction project was facing a significant budget blowout two years after it was unveiled.

item.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat in Hungary election
item.description: After 16 years in power, the powerful far-right leader says he will serve in opposition, with centre-right ⁠candidate Péter Magyar's Tisza Party ​on course for a super majority.

item.title: Want a dog‑friendly workplace? Here's what you'll need to get right
item.description: How can employers design dog-friendly workplaces if they want to, and what does the law say about animals at work?

item.title: An Australian Super Rugby Pacific win at home is one thing but victory in New Zealand is another
item.description: Australian teams will only boost their chances of achieving the ultimate success in Super Rugby Pacific if they conquer the challenge of beating their New Zealand opponents across the Tasman.

item.title: Youth mental health centre to close amid Kiama Council budget crisis
item.description: A NSW South Coast community says the proposed closure of a mental health and welfare centre could result in more youth suicides.

item.title: ACT family violence service warns $292m federal funding boost may not reach frontline
item.description: More than $290 million in federal funding is on the table for domestic violence services nationwide, but Canberra providers are concerned about how it will be distributed and tracked by the ACT government.

item.title: Major setback for beekeepers as varroa mite chemical resistance detected again
item.description: There are growing fears that a second varroa mite strain could enter Australia, as beekeepers lose key chemical options for controlling the tiny parasites of honey bees.

item.title: Cyclone Maila kills at least 11 people in Papua New Guinea
item.description: The Australian government will give $2.5 million to support relief and recovery efforts in Papua New Guinea and Solomon Islands after Tropical Cyclone Maila.

item.title: Rediscovered sheet music performed at 150th anniversary of Point Pass Immanuel Lutheran Church
item.description: A South Australian church celebrates 150 years with a special performance of sacred music transcribed by early-20th-century parishioners.

item.title: Coral Coast tourism hit with mass cancellations after Cyclone Narelle
item.description: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference", as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

item.title: Wreck Bay Aboriginal council to take on sole management of Booderee National Park in Jervis Bay
item.description: The Wreck Bay Aboriginal Community Council plans to take on sole management of Booderee National Park In May 2028.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz blockade will cut off Iran's ports. Here's what that means
item.description: US president Donald Trump has ordered his nation's military to cut off all Iranian ports from maritime traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most vital shipping routes.

item.title: Man who pleaded guilty to NSW surfer's unlawful death gives evidence during sentencing
item.description: A man has admitted to stripping a prominent Sydney surfer naked and shaking him by his ankles in an attempt to humiliate him before causing his unlawful death.

item.title: Prime minister names head of navy Mark Hammond as new defence chief
item.description: Vice Admiral Mark Hammond, who has spent 40 years in the navy, will replace current defence force chief David Johnston when he retires in July.

item.title: Record crowd for AFL Gather Round in South Australia as premier warns of 'lite' interstate version
item.description: South Australia's premier says he is "trying to hold the line" as Gather Round negotiations continue, with the 2026 event attracting record crowds.

item.title: Veteran Victorian ministers stand down ahead of November state election
item.description: Long serving Labor MPs Mary-Anne Thomas, Danny Pearson and Gayle Tierney have announced they will not contest the 2026 state election.

item.title: Five of our favourite soup recipes for cool nights
item.description: Looking for new soup recipes as the weather cools? From minestrone to hot and sour dumplings, we have you covered.

item.title: Witnesses say Cameron Duce kicked in head during alleged fatal assault on Gold Coast
item.description: Multiple witnesses told a committal hearing Gold Coast man Cameron Duce was kicked in the head in the late-night brawl.

item.title: Donald Trump's Strait of Hormuz blockade could further undermine global economic confidence
item.description: Donald Trump's planned United States naval blockade of Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz reveals the president once again playing loose with the fortunes of financial markets and the global economy as he struggles to find a way out of the war that he and Israel started.

item.title: At this Canberra farmers' market, producers are making tough choices amid rising fuel prices
item.description: As fuel prices remain high amid the war in the Middle East, growers and fishers are among those having to make tough decisions to remain viable.

item.title: Police Minister Yasmin Catley hits back at Fairfield mayor's allegation police not being deployed in Western Sydney
item.description: A $1.8 million plaza closed by council has become a flashpoint in a war of words over police resources in Sydney's west.

item.title: Teenage girl who 'weaponised' stolen cars denied bail over risk to community
item.description: A 13-year-old girl is facing court over 109 charges relating to incidents including allegedly driving into a cyclist.

item.title: WA government's $1.5 billion spend on hospitals sparks questions on where money will go
item.description: The WA government is splashing an additional $1.5 billion on health, bringing the state's total hospital spend to $5.5 billion over the next four years. But where exactly will the extra money go?

item.title: National medal for backyard astronomer Chris Stockdale who helped discover 100 exoplanets
item.description: From a backyard observatory in regional Victoria, Gippsland astronomer Chris Stockdale spends his nights helping confirm distant planets by tracking tiny changes in starlight that reveal new worlds beyond our solar system.

item.title: Euphoria season 3 cast on past, present and future of hit show
item.description: Season 3 of Euphoria has premiered today as members of the cast reflect on where the show has been and where it's going.

item.title: One Nation's Cory Bernardi approaches independent SA MPs in bid to form state opposition
item.description: One Nation SA leader Cory Bernardi has approached independent MPs to make the case for his party — instead of the Liberals — to be recognised as the state's official opposition.

item.title: Tasmanian student vaccinated without consent prompts council call for state government to run program
item.description: A Tasmanian mayor is calling on the state government to take on responsibility for running school immunisation programs, after a student was vaccinated without caregiver consent.

item.title: Zak Butters referred to AFL Tribunal after allegedly asking umpire 'how much are they paying you?'
item.description: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters will front the AFL Tribunal over an exchange with umpire Nick Foot.

item.title: Justin Bieber, Sabrina Carpenter lead Coachella's weekend one with style
item.description: The world's most talked about music festival took place over the weekend, boasting a bevy of unexpected highlights.

item.title: Reptile remains believed to be a crocodile found dumped on side of Adelaide road
item.description: Reptile remains — which are believed to be from a crocodile — have been collected by authorities after they were found dumped on the side of an Adelaide road.

item.title: Federal MP Luke Gosling warns colleagues against Ben Roberts-Smith commentary
item.description: A federal MP and former commando has urged colleagues from across the aisle to refrain from speaking out about the arrest of accused war criminal Ben Roberts-Smith.

item.title: Horizontal Falls tourism shifts toward cultural experience
item.description: Tourism operators, the state government and traditional owners strike an agreement about the future of tourism at the famed Horizontal Falls in WA's Kimberley.

item.title: Monday Finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Former Darwin teacher faces trial for allegedly striking student in the face
item.description: A former Darwin teacher has pleaded not guilty to causing serious harm after allegedly striking a female student in the face and causing her to break her front teeth.

item.title: Pope Leo says he will continue to speak out against war
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Coalition immigration policy includes social media checks and binding values commitment
item.description: Social media screening of visa applicants would be ramped up under an immigration policy to be revealed by Opposition Leader Angus Taylor.

item.title: Australia's LNG export window is closing, new report suggests
item.description: The long-term outlook for Australia's liquefied natural gas exports is structurally constrained by declining demand, and the current war in the Middle East may hasten that shift away from gas, a new report claims.

item.title: Trump's defamation lawsuit against Wall Street Journal over Epstein story dismissed
item.description: The case related to a Wall Street Journal article that described a sexually suggestive letter that the newspaper said was included in a 2003 album compiled for Epstein’s 50th birthday.

item.title: Do you taste words or hear colours? Here's the neuroscience behind synaesthesia
item.description: Have you ever tasted a word, or seen colours while listening to music? If you have, you may be among the 1 to 4 per cent of people who have a fascinating trait known as synaesthesia.

item.title: How these queer couples divide the mental load in their household
item.description: After publishing our story on the four stages of the mental load, we asked to hear from queer couples about how they divide and conquer the cognitive and emotional labour at home.

item.title: Debunking four myths about Australia's fuel crisis
item.description: If you listen to increasingly shrill calls, it is time to rebuild our oil industry. While the sentiments might be well-meant, a few unfortunate facts have been overlooked.

item.title: Australian man Sam Amine wanted in Fiji over record meth import from cartel
item.description: Key insiders from Fiji's largest-ever meth bust say the drug import had alarming links to organised crime in Australia.

item.title: $5 billion in new spending on drones as Defence learns lessons from Ukraine and Iran
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles says expanding Australia's fleet of autonomous and uncrewed systems will help keep the nation safe and boost Australia's sovereign defence industry.

item.title: Terminally ill MP Emma Vulin calls for review of ban on telehealth for voluntary assisted dying consultations
item.description: A Labor politician living with MND writes to the attorney-general, calling for laws banning doctors from discussing voluntary assisted dying during telehealth appointments to be revised.

item.title: China tests a new Taiwan strategy — and a peace talk reveals the shift
item.description: It began with a dish once served to Nixon in 1972. Now Beijing is using the same stage to reshape the Taiwan question. China is advancing a slower strategy: using economic incentives and political engagement to influence Taiwan's future.

item.title: What to watch in April, from arty comedy to dark drama
item.description: Plus Nicole Kidman is an Aussie wrestler in Margo's Got Money Troubles, Baby Reindeer's Richard Gadd returns, and the reboots keep on coming with Malcolm in the Middle.

item.title: Salty Dog Hotel the target of noise complaints as performers say Tasmanian live music is under threat
item.description: With yet another venue facing complaints about noise from residents, performers say Tasmania runs the risk of becoming a dead zone for live music.

item.title: Viktor Orbán's defeat the rejection of the Donald Trump of Europe
item.description: A record number of Hungarians turned out not just to send a strong message to their far-right prime minister, but to reject Donald Trump.

item.title: Tasmania's greyhound industry rejects phase-out compensation plan
item.description: As a final decision looms for Tasmania's greyhound industry on whether it will be phased out, breeders, trainers and other industry participants reject an exit offer from the state government.

item.title: Harry and Meghan return for a 'quasi-royal' tour of Australia, with a commercial bent
item.description: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

item.title: Feral cats boom across north west Queensland.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Feral cat numbers spark bounty push across outback Queensland
item.description: Regional councils are paying for feral cats as populations boom in outback Queensland, but experts are not convinced the strategy will help.

item.title: Former One Nation candidates accuse Pauline Hanson, James Ashby of ignoring concerns about employment of Sean Black
item.description: Two former One Nation candidates have accused party leader Pauline Hanson and chief of staff James Ashby of dismissing concerns raised internally about the convicted rapist and former campaign director Sean Black.

item.title: Murri Watch service for Indigenous children in watch houses to close over loss of government funding
item.description: Murri Watch says the service closure will pose a significant risk to the safety and wellbeing of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander children and young people.

item.title: Family of Bondi Junction Westfield attack victim call for quicker movement on inquest findings
item.description: The bereaved family of one of the Bondi Junction stabbing victims says families like hers "do not need more sympathy dressed up as action" from the NSW government.

item.title: Donald Trump's 'AI Jesus' image sparks backlash as Pope Leo responds to president's attacks
item.description: Donald Trump is criticised by religious sections of his supporter base after publishing, then deleting, the image, but the US president says: "I thought it was me as a doctor."

item.title: Banks lodge hundreds of suspicious activity reports for suspected illicit tobacco operations
item.description: Australia's big banks have made hundreds of reports of suspicious activity related to illegal tobacco since the anti-money laundering regulator called for a crackdown late last year.

item.title: Iran briefing with Matthew Doran: Warning of a global food crisis and accusations of piracy as the blockade begins
item.description: The fragile truce in the Middle East and the stand-off in the Strait of Hormuz could have dire consequences for the rest of the world. Matthew Doran has your daily update on the latest in the Middle East.

item.title: Sydney's gun violence claiming innocent lives
item.description: Sydney's underworld used to operate by a code, but those unspoken rules have come undone. In today's gangland wars, violence is spilling into suburbia, and anyone can become collateral damage.

item.title: Bundjalung elder's mission to find breastplate stolen from Lismore Museum
item.description: An Aboriginal breastplate given to Jack Kapeen in 1865 disappeared from a Lismore museum about 100 years later, only to be sold at auction in 2011.

item.title: NT Indigenous mining royalty fund AINT's investment strategy questioned
item.description: Indigenous people in the NT are being offered the chance to tap into a $680-million royalties fund, but some analysts worry the money is being spent too cautiously.

item.title: Philippines accuses China of dumping cyanide in South China Sea
item.description: Manila has accused China of poisoning waters around a ship where it has marines stationed, but Beijing has dismissed the allegations.

item.title: Electronics seized after reports of hidden cameras in Horsham Town Hall change rooms
item.description: Victorian police have seized electronic devices after reports of hidden cameras in change rooms at Horsham Town Hall.

item.title: St Kilda's Lance Collard to receive AFL sanction after being found guilty of homophobic slur
item.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard will learn via an AFL hearing how heavy a suspension he will receive after being found guilty of using a homophobic slur towards a VFL opponent.

item.title: California Democrat Eric Swalwell to resign from Congress after sexual assault allegations
item.description: Eric Swalwell, a California Democrat, says he will resign from Congress following multiple sexual assault allegations.

item.title: Police thank Nannup community after 87-year-old bushwalker rescued
item.description: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

item.title: Gout Gout's 200m record showed a maturing runner, but Australian fans must be patient
item.description: Gout Gout was on the lips of every athletics fan across the globe on the weekend, but elements of his record-breaking run need to be treated with caution.

item.title: Ukulele players strum their way to better mental health
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sunshine Coast ukulele club's 600 members may be strumming their way to better health
item.description: From a small gathering to a packed RSL, this Queensland ukulele club has found its rhythm, and a brain researcher says playing could offer its members a multitude of health benefits.

item.title: Australian paceman Mitchell Starc named leading men's cricketer of the year by Wisden
item.description: Australia's Ashes ace Mitchell Starc is named the leading men's cricketer of the year by the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack after taking 55 wickets in 2025.

item.title: Qantas cancels flights between Adelaide and Mount Gambier amid fuel costs
item.description: Qantas will no longer fly to South Australia's largest regional city, citing fuel costs and declining demand for flights to Mount Gambier.

item.title: Spanish prime minister's wife charged with corruption after two-year probe
item.description: Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez’s wife, Begona Gomez, is formally charged with corruption after a two-year criminal investigation.

item.title: From gay rights to prisoner voting, the High Court helped decide the way we live and love
item.description: From love and freedom to native title and the right to vote, these are some of the High Court cases that have helped define our nation, and the ordinary people behind them.

item.title: Qantas cuts domestic flight capacity and raises fares as fuel costs could blow out to $3.3b
item.description: Qantas will cut domestic flights due to higher fuel costs and the uncertainty of the Middle East war, as it flags as much as $800 million in extra fuel costs.

item.title: Therapeutic Goods Administration issues safety alert amid explosion of illegal peptide use
item.description: The medicines regulator issues a health alert about the risks of using unregulated peptides amid a surge in the use of potentially unsafe injectables.

item.title: Sydney summers have increased in length by nearly 50 days since 1990, study finds
item.description: For every 10 years since 1990, Sydneysiders have experienced an extra 15 days of summer, new research has found.

item.title: Fuel crisis hits WA tourist route as Jetstar suspends Sydney-Busselton flights
item.description: Rising fuel costs due to the war in Iran see Qantas suspend Jetstar flights between Sydney and Busselton.

item.title: Super Netball round-up: Expansion pushed back and the growing trend to call greats out of retirement
item.description: Two new teams were expected to join the league in 2027 to make Super Netball a 10-team competition, but slow broadcast negotiations are holding up the process.

item.title: Trailblazing former NT Labor senator Trish Crossin dies aged 70
item.description: A former Arnhem Land schoolteacher who blazed a trail as the Northern Territory’s first female senator, Trish Crossin, has died at age 70.

item.title: New ministers appointed to Jacinta Allan's cabinet in Victoria after resignations
item.description: Four Labor MPs have been promoted to the Victorian government's ministry, with one of the incoming ministers defending her past support of disgraced CFMEU head John Setka.

item.title: How I've learnt to love my asymmetrical body after cancer
item.description: I decided to keep my healthy breast, opting against reconstruction. It means I'm now what's known in the community as a uniboober.

item.title: Man charged with breaching Queensland hate speech laws tells court he wishes to plead insanity
item.description: Jim Dowling is charged with one count of publicly displaying a prohibited expression after displaying a sign reading "from the river to the sea, Brisbane will be free of Boeing."

item.title: How Wests Tigers veteran Alex Twal defied the odds to transform his NRL career
item.description: For years, Alex Twal was best known for the things he didn't do but, after helping the Tigers surge up the ladder, the cult hero forward has become one of the most improved players in the NRL.

item.title: Palestinian rights groups file legal action over Australia's arms exports to Israel
item.description: A group of Palestinian human rights organisations launch legal action against the defence minister seeking transparency over Australia's arms exports to Israel.

item.title: Police weigh up charges over alleged Islamophobic attack
item.description: Police say if there is sufficient evidence, a man accused of attacking people at a community dinner could be charged under hate speech laws.

item.title: States to reveal if they support privatisation of Big Bash League clubs, with possible IPL part-ownership
item.description: Cricket Australia will know in the coming days if the states are happy to privatise their Big Bash League clubs, a call that may see them become part-owned by Indian Premier League franchises.

item.title: The Zak Butters and Nick Foot case has shown the AFL needed to protect its umpire from conflict of interest concerns
item.description: The AFL gambled that concerns over a conflict of interest with one of its umpires wouldn't be an issue, but that call has come back to bite them — and it's not the umpire's fault.

item.title: Prince Harry and Meghan begin first leg of Australia visit in Melbourne
item.description: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have begun their multi-day visit to Australia in Melbourne, meeting with patients at a children's hospital.

item.title: Man accused in Molotov cocktail attack on OpenAI CEO's home charged with attempted murder
item.description: A man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at the home of OpenAI's CEO is charged with attempted murder. Authorities say he had written about AI's purported risk to humanity and "our impending extinction".

item.title: INXS is out, Phil Collins in as Rock & Roll Hall of Fame 2026 inductees announced
item.description: INXS has missed out on landing a place in the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame with 2026 inductees including Phil Collins, Queen Latifah and Fela Kuti.

item.title: Scientists confirm skull found in Gippsland cave a century ago belongs to giant echidna
item.description: A fossil hidden in a museum collection for 119 years has revealed giant echidnas once roamed Victoria's east, filling a major gap in Australia's prehistoric record.

item.title: Inquest application dismissed over death of Flinders Island man struck by police car
item.description: A coroner dismisses an inquest application from the wife of a Flinders Island man who was fatally struck by a police car after he had a night out at a local pub and left on foot.

item.title: WA government says it has secured 4 million litres of diesel
item.description: The diesel is set to arrive in Western Australia "in the coming weeks" as part of a strategic stockpile to address acute, localised fuel shortages across the state — but it's already been dismissed as "woefully inadequate".

item.title: Wallaroos forced to take refuge inside US hotel amid approaching tornado
item.description: The Wallaroos' preparations for the Pacific Four Series are dealt an unexpected blow as a warning alarm sounds inside the team hotel in Kansas City.

item.title: Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre could be converted into prison
item.description: The WA government flags the possible conversion of the Bullsbrook COVID quarantine centre into a prison amid an "unprecedented" rise in the number of inmates being crammed into cells across the state.

item.title: Man charged over alleged murder of Hayley McClenahan-Ernst in Sydney's west
item.description: The former partner of Hayley McClenahan-Ernst has been charged with her murder, nine-years after the pregnant mother's suspicious death in Sydney's west.

item.title: Man allegedly caught on camera kicking a dog to the head says the vision was 'doctored'
item.description: A northern suburbs man says he had been bitten and was acting in self-defence when he kicked a dog that was tethered to the tray of a ute in a Bunnings car park.

item.title: Colombia approves plan to cull roaming hippos linked to Pablo Escobar
item.description: Colombian officials have authorised a plan to cull dozens of hippos, years after notorious drug lord Pablo Escobar brought in the first ones.

item.title: Financial, environmental and safety issues emerge at Nathan River Resources' Roper Bar mine
item.description: Nathan River Resources has stood down more than 40 workers since Good Friday. Now financial, environmental and safety issues at the mine are being raised, as the NT government is accused of weak regulation.

item.title: NSW energy minister says 'difficult trade-offs' necessary in New England Renewable Energy Zone
item.description: The New South Wales energy minister has met with disgruntled landowners affected by the New England Renewable Energy Zone.

item.title: Wanted man found injured, detained by Bray Park residents, dies in hospital
item.description: A man wanted by NSW Police dies in hospital from injuries sustained after being detained by two men near Murwillumbah.

item.title: WNBA draft 2026: Aussie duo Saffron Shiels and Manuela Puoch chosen as Dallas Wings select Azzi Fudd with top pick
item.description: Saffron Shiels and Manuela Puoch continue the influx of Australians at the WNBA draft as Dallas pairs Azzi Fudd with her college teammate Paige Bueckers.

item.title: Butters found guilty of abusing umpire as Port claims 'glaring hole in the evidence'
item.description: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters is found guilty of umpire abuse at the AFL Tribunal, despite strongly denying allegations he asked umpire Nick Foot "how much are they paying you?".

item.title: Fresh warning about using superannuation on health treatments after 100 complaints to regulator
item.description: The healthcare regulator and tax office are cracking down on health practitioners who help patients access their superannuation early after one Perth doctor signed off on a $20,000 withdrawal for liposuction.

item.title: Energy analysts raise concerns on fuel excise cut, divided on whether it will 'temper' inflation
item.description: Some energy analysts are questioning the federal government's temporary tax cuts on fuel, warning that lower prices may work against Australia's long-term needs and goals.

item.title: Sydney community radio station 2SER faces uncertain future as university funding dries up
item.description: 2SER, which was jointly owned by the University of Technology Sydney and Macquarie University, has given many aspiring broadcasters their start in the industry.

item.title: Court told 'hundreds' of croc eggs allegedly stolen from Kakadu by Matt Wright's former pilot
item.description: Four men who were allegedly involved in a "sophisticated" operation to steal "hundreds" of crocodile eggs from Kakadu National Park have faced court in Darwin.

item.title: Zak Butters asked 'why did you pay that?' Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says
item.description: Port Adelaide chairman David Koch says the AFL club will back Zak Butters "to the hilt" amid dispute over what was said to an umpire during Sunday night's loss against St Kilda.

item.title: Harvey Weinstein to face retrial on New York rape charge
item.description: Former Hollywood magnate Harvey Weinstein is again going on trial on a rape charge in New York City. He will remain imprisoned for other offences regardless of the verdict.

item.title: Residents near Adelaide cement plant raise concerns about more plastic being burnt for fuel
item.description: Residents living near a large cement plant in Adelaide are concerned about the owner wanting to burn more plastic as fuel to replace natural gas.

item.title: Court hears alleged planning behind 2023 cafe shooting of underworld figure Gavin Preston
item.description: Prosecutors allege two men were chauffeured back to NSW after conducting a contract killing of the Melbourne underworld figure in 2023.

item.title: Ruling expected in Gina Rinehart's bitter legal feud against Wright Prospecting over mining riches
item.description: Gina Rinehart, the wealthiest person in Australia, expects a judgment to be handed down today in an explosive court battle with the family of her father's former business partner over iron ore royalties.

item.title: Queensland inquiry into child safety hears department's goals 'hamstrung' by underfunding
item.description: An inquiry has heard ambitious goals to improve Queensland's child safety department were unrealistic as it struggled to recover from pressures caused by the COVID pandemic.

item.title: Coalition's new migration plan faces practical questions
item.description: The Coalition's new migration policy aims to increase screening of visa applicants and "boot out" people who do not abide by Australia's values, but questions remain about how the plan would work in practice.

item.title: ASIC investigating 'complex web of private companies' in Sanjeev Gupta's empire amid growing worker anxiety
item.description: After years of financial red flags, corporate watchdog ASIC confirms it has multiple investigations underway into Sanjeev Gupta's troubled empire, following multiple collapses that have left hundreds of workers facing an uncertain future.

item.title: Inquiry hears Parkes school expects to lose students if waste incinerator is built
item.description: A school in the middle of a $22-million master plan for its growing enrolment numbers is worried the buildings will not be needed if a waste to energy plant is approved in the town.

item.title: Man behind four threatening hate letters to Lakemba Mosque, politicians given corrections order
item.description: A man who sent threatening letters to a Sydney mosque was "drinking himself to sleep" and "fell into some type of internet rabbit hole", a court hears.

item.title: St Kilda forward Lance Collard receives nine-week suspension over homophobic slur to VFL opponent
item.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard is suspended after a marathon AFL Tribunal hearing with a deliberation of over 90 minutes.

item.title: Humpback whale freed after being stranded on sandbar at Wallis Lake on NSW mid north coast
item.description: A whale stranded on a sandbar in Wallis Lake on the NSW mid north coast has been freed by marine animal rescue teams.

item.title: WA government offers FluMist nasal spray vaccine free to children
item.description: Western Australia is offering free needle-free flu vaccines for children in a bid to curb declining inoculation rates and prevent another deadly influenza season.

item.title: Gold Coast court hears conflicting witness accounts over alleged fatal assault of Cameron Duce
item.description: A court has heard conflicting witness accounts of an alleged Gold Coast assault that left 22-year-old Cameron Duce dead.

item.title: Teacher charged with breaking student's teeth takes stand in own defence in Supreme Court trial
item.description: A former Darwin teacher who allegedly struck a female student in the face has taken the stand in the Northern Territory Supreme Court, conceding he was "angry" after she swore at him.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Cartel Paradise: A special investigation into the Pacific's drug superhighway
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Founder of China property giant Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
item.description: Hui Ka Yan's plea comes after three years in detention, while Evergrande's liquidators are battling in court outside mainland China to freeze the offshore assets of the founder and his ex-spouse over billions of US dollars in dividends and remuneration.

item.title: Earthquake south of Orange shakes homes over hundreds of kilometres
item.description: The NSW Central West has been hit by the largest earthquake ever recorded in the region.

item.title: Perth man on trial for alleged rape, assault of several women
item.description: Benjamin Bruce, 26, appeared before the WA District Court on charges including sexual penetration without consent and assault against several women.

item.title: IMF warns of inflation surge, growth slump if Strait of Hormuz disruptions continue
item.description: The International Monetary Fund paints three economic outlook scenarios that each get progressively worse depending on how long Middle East energy supplies are disrupted.

item.title: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada as Liberals win three by-elections
item.description: The Canadian prime minister said voters had "placed their trust" in his government and its "clear understanding of what this moment demands".

item.title: Live: Trump says peace talks could restart in days as blockade 'completely' halts Iranian maritime trade
item.description: US President Donald Trump has said talks to end the Iran war could resume in Pakistan in the coming days, following the collapse of negotiations at the weekend. Follow live.

item.title: Trump lashes out at European ally Meloni amid tensions over pope and NATO
item.description: The US president says Giorgia Meloni "does not care" about Iran getting a nuclear weapon, after the Italian PM defended the pope and cancelled a military deal with Israel.

item.title: Travel quiz, April 15: We're going on a trip — don't forget your toothbrush
item.description: What are the pyramids made out of? And which Caribbean island doesn't appear in the Beach Boys song Kokomo?

item.title: Undertone's director taps into our fear of things that go bump in the night
item.description: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

item.title: Should you turn off your laptop while commuting?
item.description: While modern laptops are good at saving battery life and keeping cool, extended periods in sleep mode could still be putting a strain on them over time.

item.title: Users left fearing death as fake Ozempic weight loss drugs flood UK black market
item.description: Aisleyne Horgan-Wallace lay on her bed, wincing in pain, convinced she was about to die after injecting herself with a cheap, unregulated weight loss drug.

item.title: Biofuels could bolster Australia's fuel security but right now, we're exporting what we need to make them
item.description: Australia controls less than 20 per cent of our liquid fuel supply and relies on imports for the other 80-plus per cent we need to keep the nation moving. But proponents of biofuels argue it does not need to be this way.

item.title: Horsham Town Hall postpones performances after hidden cameras found in change rooms
item.description: An investigation into hidden cameras in change rooms has forced the Horsham Town Hall to postpone events in western Victoria.

item.title: The outback jobs disappearing as tourists can no longer afford fuel to travel
item.description: From roadhouses to stations, outback workers are losing their jobs as tourists stay away. Skyrocketing fuel prices, as well as concerns around the availability of fuel in remote locations, are causing a 76 per cent loss of revenue for tourist-dependent businesses.

item.title: Uber Eats and DoorDash raise concerns over proposed laws to require e-bike riders to hold licence
item.description: Food delivery giant Uber Eats hits out at the Queensland government's push to introduce licensing mandates for e-bike and e-scooter users.

item.title: Ombudsman report into interaction between Queensland Health and boys with disabilities makes 13 recommendations
item.description: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.

item.title: More than 250 missing after boat with Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals sinks in Andaman Sea
item.description: The UN says the boat sank with about 250 people on board, including children, in the Andaman Sea "due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding".

item.title: El Niño in 2026 could be one of the strongest on record — what does it mean for Australia's weather?
item.description: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly, thanks to the probable emergence of an El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires could be on the way.

item.title: Gosford Hospital leading Australia with new lifesaving stroke prevention surgery from US
item.description: A NSW hospital becomes the first in Australia to offer a new stroke-prevention treatment that has been available in the United States for a decade.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon peace talks begin but Hezbollah won't be held to any deal. What you need to know on Iran today
item.description: One week into the ceasefire, two sets of peace talks have taken place but the only commitment is to talk more.

item.title: Flood watches issued as former Cyclone Maila brings days of heavy rain
item.description: Minor flood watches have been issued for several Far North Queensland catchments as former Tropical Cyclone Maila delivers sustained heavy rainfall, with forecasters warning transport routes could be disrupted.

item.title: Victorian opposition announces bail, sentencing policy including 'one strike' bail rule
item.description: The Victorian opposition has pledged to expand the government's "adult time for violent crime" laws and introduce a "one-strike" bail rule that would see re-offenders automatically denied bail.

item.title: Thousands of NSW students embrace AI to learn but environmental, creativity concerns remain
item.description: Thousands of students are using AI to transform the way they learn, but many remain concerned about what the technology means for the environment, jobs and creativity.

item.title: Australia's Cameron Green' poor IPL season continues in Kolkata Knight Riders' loss to Chennai Super Kings
item.description: Cameron Green has suffered another challenging outing in the Indian Premier League as he struggles to live up to his tag as the most expensive overseas player.

item.title: NT Aboriginal affairs framework released as territory goes backwards on Closing the Gap targets
item.description: The Northern Territory government has released its Aboriginal Affairs framework as the territory goes backwards on several Closing the Gap targets.

item.title: Coroner calls for more e-scooter safety awareness after 14-year-old's death in country Victoria
item.description: A coroner calls for increased public safety campaigns after a 14-year-old boy died after colliding with a ute while riding an e-scooter in regional Victoria.

item.title: Your brain explained: Why it's getting harder to pay attention
item.description: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

item.title: Live: Chalmers admits budget won't be as planned but still 'ambitious'
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers says the budget he'll hand down next month is not what he initially planned, due to the Iran war. Follow live.

item.title: NFL reporter Dianna Russini resigns from The Athletic days after Mike Vrabel photos are published
item.description: The Athletic's NFL reporter Dianna Russini resigns despite denying anything untoward in photos with Patriots coach Mike Vrabel.

item.title: Platypus spotted outside hotel in Marian, Queensland
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Scientists push to have platypus listed as 'vulnerable' on conservation register
item.description: A lack of hard data on platypus numbers holds back a Queensland-led effort to list the iconic monotreme as "vulnerable" on the national Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation register.

item.title: JCP Youth 'BEAST' outreach program scrutinised over funding and model of care
item.description: The taxpayer-funded outreach organisation JCP Youth runs the "flagship" BEAST program to divert at-risk youths, but a parliamentary committee has concerns over its evidence base and lack of a tender process for funding.

item.title: Live: ASX follows Wall Street higher, oil prices sink on hope of further US-Iran peace talks
item.description: The Australian share market has risen in morning trade as global investors bet on the US-Iran war being over within months, despite fuel shortages, higher inflation and a possible economic slowdown. Follow live.

item.title: Heavyweight boxer Oleksandr Usyk puts WBC title on line against kickboxer Rico Verhoeven
item.description: World heavyweight boxing champion Oleksandr Usyk says it is time to do what he wants ahead of his title bout against a kickboxer who has only had one boxing fight.

item.title: Tributes flow for 'extraordinary' Robe sculptor and horsewoman Patricia Moseley
item.description: Across dusty outback tracks to the promenades of Adelaide, sculptor Pat Moseley turned limestone and bronze into the faces of legends.

item.title: After backlash over World Cup ticket prices, FIFA provides more expensive options
item.description: After a backlash over ticket prices, FIFA puts up a new option for fans — and it is even more expensive.

item.title: Family hopes epilepsy Senate inquiry will shine light on funding gaps
item.description: Four-year-old Leo has a rare form of epilepsy, and his family has turned to creative ways to fund help for him. An advocacy group says others are in the same boat, and it hopes a new inquiry will help.

item.title: Lebanon, Israel agree to direct negotiations after Washington talks
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agree to direct negotiations following talks in Washington hailed by US Secretary of State Marco Rubio as a "historic opportunity" for peace.

item.title: Man buried in shallow grave at Ouyen was victim of recent sheep thefts, family says
item.description: The daughter of a murdered Ouyen farmer says her father had recently made reports to police about sheep thefts from his farm.

item.title: Outback nurse Hamish Briggs captures life working in remote WA on social media
item.description: Amid chronic nursing shortages in Western Australia's regions, one nurse highlights the profession's unique benefits and captures his experience on social media.

item.title: PSG beats Liverpool, Atlético Madrid downs Barcelona to reach Champions League semifinals
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain and Atlético Madrid advance to the last four of the Champions League, as Liverpool's rough season continues with a key injury that deals a blow to France's World Cup chances.

item.title: NSW pharmacists will soon be able to prescribe the contraceptive pill
item.description: Patients will no longer have to visit the doctor to be dispensed the contraceptive pill, under a new pharmacy prescription scheme.

item.title: Lance Collard's hearing and monster sanction leaves no winners, as queer community further bruised
item.description: Lance Collard is handed a monster suspension after being found guilty of using a homophobic slur in the first AFL disciplinary hearing.

item.title: Recovery crew opens capsule carrying Artemis II crew
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: New footage captures moment Artemis II crew welcomed home
item.description: New footage shows the moment a recovery crew opens the hatch of Artemis II's Orion capsule and welcomes its crew members home after their mission around the Moon.

item.title: Virgin to trim domestic flights ahead of fuel costs rising by up to $40 million
item.description: Australian airlines are halting entire routes and merging slots to ensure planes are full, as the aviation industry battles soaring jet fuel costs and likely supply uncertainty over the coming months.

item.title: Pub revival underway in historic outback Queensland town of Barcaldine
item.description: A revival is brewing in Barcaldine, with the renovation and reopening of two pubs the talk of the town.

item.title: Warnings of aftershocks after record NSW earthquake
item.description: Listen to ABC News interviews and commentary and analysis from radio programs like AM, PM and The World Today.

item.title: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan reveals the cabinet that she will take to the November election
item.description: More responsibilities for Attorney-General Sonya Kilkenny and a new cost of living portfolio are among major changes in state cabinet.

item.title: Media head Antony Catalano's wife flagged down passing car for help after alleged assault, court told
item.description: The wife of media executive Antony Catalano fled the couple's luxury apartment and flagged down a passing car for help following a sustained attack by her husband that caused hospitalisation, police allege.

item.title: Prince Harry shows off footy skills at men's mental health event in Melbourne's west
item.description: The Duke of Sussex was joined by Western Bulldogs players at a Movember event in Melbourne's west.

item.title: Zak Butters verbal abuse verdict raises questions about AFL's 'conflict of interest' with gambling
item.description: The reporting of Port Adelaide player Zak Butters for umpire abuse sparks debate about whether officials should be involved with betting and if better audio recordings should be available from games.

item.title: Super Rugby Pacific's Moana Pasifika say 2026 season will be team's last due to finances and other challenges
item.description: Citing financial, operational, and strategic barriers that have made continuing unviable, battling Super Rugby Pacific outfit Moana Pasifika reveal they will disband at season's end.

item.title: King Charles to woo Trump and restore 'closest of friendships' on US visit
item.description: King Charles will have tea and a private meeting with US President Donald Trump during his US state visit later this month, with trips to Washington, New York and Virginia.

item.title: Newmont's Cadia gold mine suspends underground operations after earthquake in Orange
item.description: All underground workers at Newmont's Cadia gold mine are brought to the surface as inspections are carried out following an earthquake.

item.title: Latitude Financial fined almost $4 million for breaching Australia's spam laws again
item.description: Latitude Financial has been fined millions of dollars for a repeated breach of spam laws, including promoting credit cards without giving recipients a way to opt-out.

item.title: How white should your teeth actually be?
item.description: Experts say there are many factors that influence how white a tooth appears, and it's not just about whitening or what we’re eating.

item.title: Hillcrest jumping castle inquest hoped to begin this year now criminal case has concluded
item.description: After being delayed while waiting for the criminal case to run, a court hears there is "hope" the inquest into the 2021 deaths of six Tasmanian school children will start this year.

item.title: Man, 26, found dead following violent disturbance in Milingimbi
item.description: A man has died after a violent disturbance in Milingimbi yesterday, in which police allege some people were armed with edged weapons.

item.title: Police arrest 18yo man over armed home invasion on Gold Coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Victorian independent candidates win High Court battle over cap on political donations
item.description: Two independents have successfully argued Victorian political donation law was unfair and risked allowing wealthy voices to drown out others.

item.title: Frenchman wins Picasso painting worth 1 million euros
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Frenchman scoops up 1-million-euro Picasso painting in Paris raffle
item.description: A man from Paris has won a Picasso painting worth 1 million euros ($1.6 million) with a 100-euro raffle ticket.

item.title: Austin Ballard committed to stand trial over alleged fatal kick during late-night brawl
item.description: The man accused of delivering the blow that later killed a 22-year-old on the Gold Coast has been committed to stand trial.

item.title: Property developer allegedly criticised mayor's age and intellect in harrassment case
item.description: A Southern Highlands developer facing harassment allegations says legal action is politically motivated, while police allege a pattern of emails and conduct targeting senior council officials.
